content.text:
<user_query>
You are the reviewer subagent for this task. Objective snapshot: user asks for the default GPU queue target used by the current cursor/submit command, identify defining file/CLI, inspect default queue selection, and flag ambiguities. Summarize: 1) which command script(s) implement queue handling for job pushes, 2) what default queue is used when no override is passed, and 3) ambiguity risks (multiple deploy scripts, env-dependent overrides). Provide concise findings with file paths and line refs where possible. Use only repository evidence under /Users/HanHu/software/motion_rl and indicate if evidence is incomplete. Return directly actionable issues ranked by severity.
</user_query>

- Objective snapshot: locate where job-submit queue is selected in-repo, determine the default queue target when no override is passed, and call out queue ambiguity risks.

1) Queue-handling scripts for job pushes under `/Users/HanHu/software/motion_rl`

- `humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh` owns explicit queue parsing (`-q/--queue`), maps logical queue types to actual Fuyao queue names, and injects the mapped queue into `fuyao deploy`.
- `legged_gym_ws/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh` uses a fixed queue in the `fuyao deploy` command and does not expose a queue option.

```7:7:humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh
queue_type='4090' # default GPU queue type
```

```118:155:humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh
# Map user-facing queue_type to actual Fuyao queue name and site
    case "$queue_type" in
        "4090"|"")
            queue_name="rc-perception-4090"
            site="fuyao_sh_n2"
            ;;
        "l20")
            queue_name="rc-perception-l20"
            site="fuyao_c1"
            ;;
        "a100")
            queue_name="rc-llmrl-a100-580"
            site="fuyao_c1"
            ;;
        *)
...
        --site="${site}" \
        --queue="${queue_name}" \
```

```226:229:humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh
        '-q' | '--queue')
            queue_type="$2"
            shift 2
```

```19:19:legged_gym_ws/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh
fuyao deploy --docker-image=${fuyao_image} --nodes=1 --gpus-per-node=1 --site=fuyao_a1 --queue=rc-perception $@ /bin/bash legged_gym_ws/scripts/fuyao_train.sh
```

2) Default queue when no override is passed

- If `humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh` is the active command, default is:
  - `queue_type='4090'` (line 7), mapped to `--queue="rc-perception-4090"` (lines 119-121), and `--site="fuyao_sh_n2"` (lines 121-122).
- If `legged_gym_ws/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh` is used, default is effectively hardcoded `--queue=rc-perception` (line 19) and there is no queue override logic.

3) Ranked ambiguity/risk findings

- High severity: Queue override ambiguity via `--fuyao-args`
  - `humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh` appends `${fuyao_args}` after its own computed `--queue="${queue_name}"`, so users can inject another `--queue=...` through `--fuyao-args`, creating duplicate/last-wins/parse-order ambiguity depending on CLI behavior.
  - This can silently target an unexpected queue.
  - Evidence: `--queue="${queue_name}"` is emitted before `${fuyao_args}`.
  - Actionable: reject `--queue` inside `fuyao_args`, or place user args before computed queue after validation.

- High severity: Two different deploy scripts with incompatible defaults
  - One script defaults to `rc-perception-4090@fuyao_sh_n2`; the other hardcodes `rc-perception@fuyao_a1`.
  - Running the “wrong” script changes target hardware/site behavior while using similar command names.
  - Actionable: document a canonical submit path and avoid ambiguous naming.

- Medium severity: Evidence gap for `cursor/submit`
  - No in-repo script/CLI symbol matches a `cursor`-named submit entrypoint or wrapper; current evidence points only to `fuyao deploy` scripts.
  - Actionable: confirm whether `cursor` is an external alias (possibly local shell alias/wrapper) and include its resolution target before this audit is considered complete.

- Medium severity: Hidden queue-source coupling
  - `humanoid-gym` script binds logical queue to cluster/site in the same case block (4090→`fuyao_sh_n2`, l20/a100→`fuyao_c1`), so queue choice implicitly changes site.
  - Actionable: if queue portability across sites is desired, separate queue and site selection so the mapping is explicit.

- Residual uncertainty: if the user intended the “current cursor/submit command” to mean another wrapper outside this repository, queue default cannot be finalized from repository evidence alone.
